title: rich velvety comforting mediterranean rice flour pancakes
description: a rich and comforting dish featuring mediterranean-inspired rice flour pancakes served in a fondue pot, perfect for a low-calorie-friendly snack

ingredients.name: lemon zest
ingredients.notes: freshly grated from a medium lemon

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed from a medium lemon

ingredients.name: garlic powder
ingredients.notes: substitute with onion powder if needed

instructions: Preheat a non-stick fondue pot over low heat on your stovetop.
In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour, grated zucchini, crumbled feta cheese, and garlic powder.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, low-fat plain yogurt, lemon zest, and lemon juice.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Brush the interior of the fondue pot with a small amount of olive oil to prevent sticking.
Carefully pour the pancake batter into the fondue pot and cook, covered, for 4-5 minutes or until the bottoms are lightly golden.
Loosen the edges of the pancakes with a spatula and carefully flip them over.
Cook for an additional 4-5 minutes or until the other side is also lightly golden.
Serve immediately, garnished with a sprinkle of salt and a dollop of yogurt if desired.
Remove the fondue pot from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
Be careful when handling the hot fondue pot.
Wash your hands before and after handling raw eggs and zucchini for food safety.
